mission.title: backstage: 🚀 Feature: catalog export
mission.description: 🚀 Feature: catalog export. Requested by 15+ users.

steps.title: Check current backstage setup
steps.description:
Verify your backstage version and configuration:
```bash
npx backstage-cli info
```
This feature requires a working backstage installation.

steps.title: Review backstage configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant backstage configuration:
### 🔖 Feature description

Add a button to the catalog index page that allows you to export the data to for instance a csv or excel sheet.

Complicating factor: https://github.com/backstage/backstage/issues/12247.

steps.title: Apply the fix for 🚀 Feature: catalog export
steps.description:
Added streaming CSV and JSON export support to CatalogIndexPage, with full support for both the legacy and new frontend systems.

CatalogIndexPage now offers an optional export feature that lets users export their current catalog view, including all active filters, to a file. Exports are streamed
```yaml
New frontend system (app-config.yaml):
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "🚀 Feature: catalog export" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Added streaming CSV and JSON export support to CatalogIndexPage, with full support for both the legacy and new frontend systems.

CatalogIndexPage now offers an optional export feature that lets users export their current catalog view, including all active filters, to a file. Exports are streamed directly to disk using async generators, avoiding buffering the full dataset in memory.

prerequisites.description: A working backstage installation or development environment.
